weaknesses: The annual cost extrapolation ($89K) assumes 10K tasks/day at GPT-4o prices with no batching or caching -- a reviewer may question this scenario's realism or ask for sensitivity analysis

The DIT crossover thresholds (0.35, 0.55, 0.70) are stated as facts in the introduction but derived empirically in Section 5 -- a forward reference or softening ('our experiments reveal') would be more precise
The p=0.41 significance value for trivial-task debate-flat gap is a strong claim for an introduction; the permutation test details live in Section 5
Figure 1 is referenced but the figure itself is a placeholder -- the conceptual diagram must be created before submission
The contributions list has 5 items; NeurIPS reviewers sometimes prefer 3-4 tightly scoped contributions over a longer list
revision_priority: Verify the annual cost figure ($89K) is consistent with the cost model in Section 3 and the results in Section 5
Soften the DIT crossover claims to indicate they are empirical findings previewed here and substantiated in Section 5
Consider consolidating contributions 3 and 4 (empirical evaluation + Pareto frontier analysis) into a single contribution to tighten the list
Ensure Figure 1 placeholder is flagged for the figure-generation pipeline
Cross-check all numbers (53.3%, 95.6%, 2016 runs, $0.023 cost/correct) against the results section for consistency
